stories.title: Roadworks across Flintshire this week: closures and traffic lights affecting routes across the county
stories.summary: A number of road closures and temporary traffic lights are in place across Flintshire this week, affecting routes in Shotton, Buckley, Connah’s Quay, Holywell, Flint, Mold and surrounding areas. The following works are recorded on Flintshire County Council’s roadworks bulletin for the week ending 26 April 2026. Drury Lane in Drury, Buckley is closed for

stories.title: Greenfield Valley parkrun invites walkers to join this Saturday’s parkwalk day
stories.summary: Greenfield Valley parkrun is welcoming walkers of all abilities this Saturday, 25 April, as part of parkwalk day — an annual event designed to encourage people to take part regardless of fitness level. The event starts at 9am at Greenfield Valley, Strand Walk, Holywell, CH8 7AN, and is open to anyone, including those who have

stories.title: Residents can question HyNet project team at Hawarden event this Wednesday
stories.summary: Residents in Flintshire can meet the team behind the HyNet CO2 pipeline at a public drop-in event in Hawarden this week, with construction work already under way across parts of the county. Liverpool Bay CCS Limited, the company building the pipeline, is holding a community information event at the Hawarden Masonic Hall on Wednesday, 22

stories.title: Shadow Chancellor of the Exchequer ducks question on HS2 funding during Llandudno visit
stories.summary: The Shadow Chancellor of the Exchequer, Mel Stride, avoided answering a direct question on HS2 funding during a visit to Llandudno on Thursday, instead blaming Welsh Government spending – despite the UK Government funding the scheme. The Local Democracy Reporting service asked the Conservative MP why High Speed 2 is still classed as an “England

stories.title: Students put Senedd hopefuls on the spot in youth-led Cambria debates
stories.summary: Learners at Coleg Cambria didn’t just attend political hustings ahead of the Senedd Elections on May 7 – they ran them. In a pair of events held at the college’s Wrexham Yale and Deeside sites, students chaired debates, set the agenda and put candidates from political parties directly on the spot.
